by_judge.judge: C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6 AT DHARWAD
D K SINGH & RAJESH RAI K
P SREE SUDHA IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 25.06.2026

narrative:
On June 25, 2026, the Karnataka High Court registered an overall hearing rate of 45.9%, successfully addressing 1,045 out of 2,275 scheduled cases across 41 judges. This performance reflects a modest increase in efficiency compared to the 44.0% hearing rate (1,142 of 2,596 cases) recorded on June 18, though it sits below the 48.9% rate achieved on June 15. 

Judicial workloads were heavily concentrated, with Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 handling the largest individual roster of 250 cases, hearing 105 (42.0%). Strong disposals were led by Justice V. Srishananda in Hall 21, who resolved 65 out of 85 cases (76.5%), and Justice Chillakur Sumalatha in Hall 25, who heard 48 out of 64 cases (75.0%). In contrast, while Justice B. M. Shyam Prasad in Hall 6 did not hear any cases from a major 92-case list, he converted 25 of 27 cases (92.6%) on a separate roster. 

From a stage perspective, Orders remained the most active phase, accounting for 794 scheduled matters and maintaining a steady 48.4% hearing rate, mirroring the 48.0% conversion rate from June 18. Preliminary Hearings saw a 49.4% conversion rate (177 of 358), whereas Final Hearings converted at a lower rate of 31.2% (115 of 369). 

In case-type distribution, Writ Petitions (WP) dominated the docket with 985 scheduled cases, securing a 46.3% hearing rate, which is an improvement over the 43.2% recorded on June 18. Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) and Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) also showed stable clearance trends, recording conversion rates of 49.4% and 56.2% respectively.
